title: blepharipappus glandulosus var. heterotrichus
intro: Blepharipappus glandulosus var. heterotrichus, also known as sticky greasyleaf, is a rare species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Blepharipappus glandulosus var. heterotrichus include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil, and it is often used in topical creams and salves to promote skin health.
